questionText: A curve has parametric equations $x = t + 1$ and $y = \dfrac{2}{t}$, where $t \neq 0$. Find a Cartesian equation of the curve, and state the value of $x$ that must be excluded.

steps.description: Make t the subject of the x equation
steps.explanation: The $x$ equation is linear, so one rearrangement gives $t$ in terms of $x$, ready to substitute into the reciprocal expression for $y$.
Replacing $t$ with $x - 1$ inside the reciprocal removes the parameter, leaving $y$ purely in terms of $x$.
This is the Cartesian equation of the curve, a reciprocal relation between $x$ and $y$.

steps.description: Find the excluded value of x
steps.explanation: Division by zero is undefined, so $x = 1$ cannot occur; it is the value that must be excluded.

steps.description: Link the exclusion to the parameter
steps.explanation: The forbidden value $x = 1$ corresponds exactly to $t = 0$, which is already excluded in the parametric definition, so the two conditions agree.
As $x$ approaches $1$ the denominator approaches zero and $|y|$ grows without limit, so the line $x = 1$ is a vertical asymptote.
For very large $|x|$ the fraction shrinks toward zero, so $y = 0$ is a horizontal asymptote of the curve.

steps.description: Check with a sample value of t
steps.explanation: At $t = 2$ the point is $(3,\,1)$, and substituting $x = 3$ into the Cartesian equation returns $y = 1$, confirming consistency.

steps.workingLatex: y = \dfrac{2}{x - 1} \text{ is } y = \dfrac{2}{x} \text{ shifted right by } 1
steps.explanation: Replacing $x$ with $x - 1$ translates the standard reciprocal curve one unit to the right, which is exactly this curve.
This is the Cartesian equation together with the excluded value, as required.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: $y = \dfrac{2}{x - 1}$, with $x \neq 1$ — the reciprocal curve $y = \dfrac{2}{x}$ translated one unit to the right.
